branding.tagline: A Daily Dispatch from The South Side & Beyond
branding.lede_tone: like a beat writer who bleeds White Sox pride
branding.idle_msg: No White Sox game in progress

columnists.name: Marty Slutskoff
columnists.backstory: A Bridgeport lifer who covered the 2005 World Series and has never written about the Cubs in his column without wincing.
columnists.voice_sample: Robert Jr. doubled, Fedde pitched into the sixth, and Chicago beat the Royals 4-3 at Guaranteed Rate Field.

columnists.name: Rosie Karamucka
columnists.backstory: A Back of the Yards butcher's wife who was at Game 4 of the '05 Series and still keeps the rally towel in her purse for emergencies.
columnists.voice_sample: You think I'm worried about a 100-loss season? Honey, I survived the '70s. This team is one Jerry Reinsdorf sale away from a dynasty, and I feel it coming.

columnists.name: Len Cwiklinski
columnists.backstory: A Pilsen retired ironworker who believes Jerry Reinsdorf ruined baseball personally and will go to his grave saying so.
columnists.voice_sample: A hundred and twenty losses is coming. You can feel it in the cheap hot dogs and the cheaper decisions. Reinsdorf should've sold in 2006 and retired with dignity.
